name: setting
summary: Full WordPress setting stack: registers the option with a default and provides a typed accessor.
description: Composite that scaffolds both the option registrar (default value applied on Ready) and a typed provider service for read/write. The provider is autowired via the DI container.
problem: You're introducing a new WordPress option and you want both the registration (with a default) and a typed accessor in one shot.
appliesWhen: You're starting fresh on a new option
You want both the default-value registrar and a typed accessor
Consumers should depend on a service rather than calling get_option directly
avoidWhen: The option already exists — use phpnomad/option-provider only
You only need defaults, no accessor — use phpnomad/option only
examples: Set up the acme_settings option with defaults and a provider
tradeoffs: Two classes plus a Ready listener registration. If your option needs Settings API integration (register_setting for REST exposure), edit the registrar after scaffolding to add it.
outputs: A getListeners() entry registering the registrar on Ready
postApply: Edit defaultValue() in both classes to return the actual default
Add typed methods on the provider as your usage solidifies
If you need Settings API integration, add register_setting() inside the registrar's handle() method

name.description: Class name shared between the registrar and the provider (PascalCase, no suffix)
name.aiHint: PascalCase. The provider class will be named {name}Provider.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register the listener in
